name: Infisical
short_desc: Open-source secrets manager — store, sync, and inject API keys / DB credentials across environments.
author: Infisical
description: Infisical is an open-source secrets management platform — a self-hostable alternative to HashiCorp Vault, Doppler, and AWS Secrets Manager. Store API keys, database credentials, and config secrets centrally, then sync them into your apps via CLI / SDK / Kubernetes operator, GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, Vercel, Netlify, AWS Secrets Manager, and 30+ more integrations. Includes secret versioning, point-in-time rollback, role-based access control, audit logs, dynamic secrets, and SSO. This package bundles the Infisical server, Postgres 14, and Redis.

form_fields.label: Encryption Key
form_fields.hint: Auto-generated 16-byte (32-hex-char) AES key used to encrypt every secret stored in the database. Don't rotate after first boot — every stored secret becomes unreadable.

form_fields.label: JWT Auth Secret
form_fields.hint: Auto-generated key Infisical uses to sign authentication JWTs. Rotating it logs every user out.

form_fields.label: Postgres Password
form_fields.hint: Auto-generated password for the bundled Postgres `infisical` user.

form_fields.label: SMTP Host
form_fields.hint: Optional. Without SMTP, Infisical can't send signup invites, password resets, or notification emails — first-boot signup will still work but team-invite flows are broken.

form_fields.label: SMTP From Address
form_fields.hint: From: address used on outbound emails (e.g. infisical@yourdomain.com).
